description: At the Lion's Club "Fishin' Hole" on the east side of Holyoke, the birds, quickly identified as empids, were taking low perches in willows, bushes, and trees near a small stream.  At times, the birds chased each other around.  Visible characteristics were large bill (relative to other empids) with entirely pale underside, complete but narrow eyering, olive-colored back, white wingbars, longish primary projection, and wide and straight-sided tail.  Each bird called "peep" multiple times.  One of the birds sang "rreeBEEa" twice.
